text: So the heavens, the earth, and everything in them were completed.
By the seventh day God had finished the work he had been doing, and on the seventh day he rested from all of it.
God blessed the seventh day and set it apart as holy, because on it he rested from all the work of creating that he had done.
This is the account of the heavens and the earth when they were created, at the time the LORD God made the earth and the heavens.
No shrub of the field had yet grown and no plant of the field had yet sprouted, because the LORD God had not sent rain on the land and there was no one to work the ground,
but a mist would rise up from the earth and water the whole surface of the ground.
Then the LORD God formed the man from the dust of the ground and breathed the breath of life into his nostrils, and the man became a living being.
The LORD God planted a garden toward the east, in Eden, and placed there the man he had formed.

The LORD God made every tree grow from the ground that was pleasant to look at and good to eat, with the tree of life in the middle of the garden, along with the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
A river flowed out of Eden to water the garden, and from there it divided into four branches.
The name of the first is the Pishon, which winds through the whole land of Havilah, where gold is found.
The gold of that land is good; bdellium resin and onyx stone are also found there.
The name of the second river is the Gihon, which winds through the whole land of Cush.
The name of the third river is the Hiddekel, which runs east of Assyria, and the fourth river is the Euphrates.
The LORD God took the man and settled him in the garden of Eden to work it and take care of it.
The LORD God commanded the man, you may freely eat from any tree in the garden,
but you must not eat from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, for on the day you eat from it, you will certainly die.
The LORD God said, it is not good for the man to be alone; I will make a companion suited to him.

Out of the ground the LORD God formed every wild animal and every bird of the sky, and brought them to the man to see what he would name them; whatever the man called each living creature became its name.
So the man named all the livestock, the birds of the sky, and the wild animals, but no companion suited to him was found.
So the LORD God caused the man to fall into a deep sleep, and while he slept, he took one of his ribs and closed up the flesh at that place.
Then the LORD God fashioned the rib he had taken from the man into a woman and brought her to him.
The man said, this one at last is bone from my bones and flesh from my flesh; she will be called Woman, because she was taken out of Man.
That is why a man leaves his father and mother and is joined to his wife, and the two become one flesh.
The man and his wife were both naked, and they felt no shame.
